tools_detail.description:
Resolves a package/product name to a Context7-compatible library ID and returns matching libraries.

You MUST call this function before 'Query Documentation' tool to obtain a valid Context7-compatible library ID UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Each result includes:
- Library ID: Context7-compatible identifier

query.description: The question or task you need help with. This is used to rank library results by relevance to what the user is trying to accomplish. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

libraryName.description: Library name to search for and retrieve a Context7-compatible library ID. Use the official library name with proper punctuation — e.g., 'Next.js' instead of 'nextjs', 'Customer.io' instead of 'customerio', 'Three.js' instead of 'threejs'.

tools_detail.name: query-docs
tools_detail.description:
Retrieves and queries up-to-date documentation and code examples from Context7 for any programming library or framework.

You must call 'Resolve Context7 Library ID' tool first to obtain the exact Context7-compatible library ID required to use this tool, UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Do not call this tool mo

libraryId.description: Exact Context7-compatible library ID (e.g., '/mongodb/docs', '/vercel/next.js', '/supabase/supabase', '/vercel/next.js/v14.3.0-canary.87') retrieved from 'resolve-library-id' or directly from user query in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version'.

query.description: The question or task you need help with, scoped to a single concept. Be specific and include relevant details, but keep each query to one topic — if the user's question spans multiple distinct concepts, make a separate call per concept instead of combining them, unless the question is about how the concepts interact. Good: 'How to set up authentication with JWT in Express.js' or 'React useEffect cleanup function examples'. Bad (too vague): 'auth' or 'hooks'. Bad (too broad): 'routing and auth and caching in Next.js'. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

tools_detail.name: generate_district_map
tools_detail.description: Generates regional distribution maps, which are usually used to show the administrative divisions and coverage of a dataset. It is not suitable for showing the distribution of specific locations, such as urban administrative divisions, GDP distribution maps of provinces and cities across the country, etc. This tool is limited to generating data maps within China.

title.description: The map title should not exceed 16 characters. The content should be consistent with the information the map wants to convey and should be accurate, rich, creative, and attractive.

name.description: Keywords for the Chinese name of an administrative region (must be within China), and must be one of China, province, city, district, or county. The name should be more specific and add attributive descriptions, for example, "西安市" is better than "西安", "杭州西湖区" is better than "西湖区". It cannot be a specific place name or a vague name, such as "其它".

style.description: Style settings.

fillColor.description: Fill color, rgb or rgba format.

colors.description: Data color list, in rgb or rgba format.

dataType.description: The type of the data value, numeric or enumeration type

dataValue.description: Data value, numeric string or enumeration string.

subdistricts.description: Sub-administrative regions are used to display the regional composition or regional distribution of related data.

name.description: Keywords for the Chinese name of an administrative region (must be within China), and must be one of China, province, city, district, or county. The name should be more specific and add attributive descriptions, for example, "西安市" is better than "西安", "杭州西湖区" is better than "西湖区". It cannot be a specific place name or a vague name, such as "其它".

dataValue.description: Data value, numeric string or enumeration string.

style.description: Style settings.

fillColor.description: Fill color, rgb or rgba format.

data.description: Administrative division data, lower-level administrative divisions are optional. There are usually two scenarios: one is to simply display the regional composition, only `fillColor` needs to be configured, and all administrative divisions are consistent, representing that all blocks are connected as one; the other is the regional data distribution scenario, first determine the `dataType`, `dataValueUnit` and `dataLabel` configurations, `dataValue` should be a meaningful value and consistent with the meaning of dataType, and then determine the style configuration. The `fillColor` configuration represents the default fill color for areas without data. Lower-level administrative divisions do not need `fillColor` configuration, and their fill colors are determined by the `colors` configuration (If `dataType` is "number", only one base color (warm color) is needed in the list to calculate the continuous data mapping color band; if `dataType` is "enum", the number of color values in the list is equal to the number of enumeration values (contrast colors)). If `subdistricts` has a value, `showAllSubdistricts` must be set to true. For example, {"title": "陕西省地级市分布图", "data": {"name": "陕西省", "showAllSubdistricts": true, "dataLabel": "城市", "dataType": "enum", "colors": ["#4ECDC4", "#A5D8FF"], "subdistricts": [{"name": "西安市", "dataValue": "省会"}, {"name": "宝鸡市", "dataValue": "地级市"}, {"name": "咸阳市", "dataValue": "地级市"}, {"name": "铜川市", "dataValue": "地级市"}, {"name": "渭南市", "dataValue": "地级市"}, {"name": "延安市", "dataValue": "地级市"}, {"name": "榆林市", "dataValue": "地级市"}, {"name": "汉中市", "dataValue": "地级市"}, {"name": "安康市", "dataValue": "地级市"}, {"name": "商洛市", "dataValue": "地级市"}]}, "width": 1000, "height": 1000}.

tools_detail.name: generate_path_map
tools_detail.description: Generate a route map to display the user's planned route, such as travel guide routes.

title.description: The map title should not exceed 16 characters. The content should be consistent with the information the map wants to convey and should be accurate, rich, creative, and attractive.

data.description: A list of keywords for the names of points of interest (POIs) in Chinese. These POIs usually contain a group of places with similar locations, so the names should be more descriptive, must adding attributives to indicate that they are different places in the same area, such as "北京市" is better than "北京", "杭州西湖" is better than "西湖"; in addition, if you can determine that a location may appear in multiple areas, you can be more specific, such as "杭州西湖的苏堤春晓" is better than "苏堤春晓". The tool will use these keywords to search for specific POIs and query their detailed data, such as latitude and longitude, location photos, etc. For example, ["西安钟楼", "西安大唐不夜城", "西安大雁塔"].

items.description: The route and places along it.

data.description: Routes, each group represents all POIs along a route. For example, [{ "data": ["西安钟楼", "西安大唐不夜城", "西安大雁塔"] }, { "data": ["西安曲江池公园", "西安回民街"] }]

tools_detail.name: generate_waterfall_chart
tools_detail.description: Generate a waterfall chart to visualize the cumulative effect of sequentially introduced positive or negative values, such as showing how an initial value is affected by a series of intermediate positive or negative values leading to a final result. Waterfall charts are ideal for financial analysis, budget tracking, profit and loss statements, and understanding the composition of changes over time

data.description: Data for waterfall chart, it should be an array of objects. Each object must contain a `category` field. For regular items, a `value` field is also required. The `isIntermediateTotal` field marks intermediate subtotals, and the `isTotal` field marks the final total. For example, [{ category: 'Initial', value: 100 }, { category: 'Increase', value: 50 }, { category: 'Subtotal', isIntermediateTotal: true }, { category: 'Decrease', value: -30 }, { category: 'Total', isTotal: true }].

tools_detail.name: get_updates
tools_detail.description: Read the stored Ninova tracking history and return recent detected changes.

tools_detail.name: get_upcoming_deadlines
tools_detail.description: Return assignments whose submission deadline is approaching based on the stored tracking snapshot.

tools_detail.name: read_page
tools_detail.description: Fetch any Ninova page and return a structured summary of text, headings, links, tables, and attachments.

tools_detail.name: crawl_course
tools_detail.description: Inventory pages and downloadable resources inside a Ninova course tree.

tools_detail.name: download_resource
tools_detail.description: Download a Ninova file or other authenticated resource to disk.

tools_detail.name: read_resource_text
tools_detail.description: Extract plain text from a Ninova PDF/DOCX/TXT (or a local path from download_resource) so the assistant can read lecture notes and assignments.

tools_detail.name: get_assignment_upload_slots
tools_detail.description: List the file slots on an assignment's 'Ödevi Yükle / OdevGonder' page, including which slots are already filled and allowed extensions.

tools_detail.name: submit_assignment
tools_detail.description: Upload a local file to one Ninova assignment slot via the official multipart form. Requires confirm=true to actually post; without it, returns a dry-run preview only. Ask the user before confirming.

tools_detail.name: snapshot_page
tools_detail.description: Save a structured snapshot of a Ninova page for later comparison.

tools_detail.name: diff_snapshot
tools_detail.description: Compare the current state of a Ninova page against a previously stored snapshot.

tools_detail.name: obs_auth_status
tools_detail.description: Check whether an OBS JWT can be obtained with the configured İTÜ credentials.

tools_detail.name: obs_get_profile
tools_detail.description: Read OBS personal/program profile. Sensitive fields are redacted unless include_sensitive=true.

tools_detail.name: obs_list_programs
tools_detail.description: List the student's OBS academic programs (e.g. undergraduate major).

tools_detail.name: obs_list_semesters
tools_detail.description: List academic semesters available in OBS for the student.

tools_detail.name: obs_get_registration_status
tools_detail.description: Read OBS registration and course-registration status (active/class level).

tools_detail.name: obs_get_advisor
tools_detail.description: Read academic advisor information from OBS.

tools_detail.name: obs_get_internships
tools_detail.description: Read internship records from OBS.

tools_detail.name: obs_get_contacts
tools_detail.description: Read contact records from OBS (phones/emails redacted by default).

tools_detail.name: obs_list_registered_courses
tools_detail.description: List courses registered in OBS for a semester (default: latest).

tools_detail.name: obs_get_course_grades
tools_detail.description: Read midterm and/or letter grades for an OBS class (by sinifId or course code).

tools_detail.name: obs_get_attendance
tools_detail.description: Read attendance for an OBS class. Includes computed absence-risk summary by default.

tools_detail.name: obs_get_schedule
tools_detail.description: Read weekly class schedule and final exam calendar for a semester.

tools_detail.name: obs_get_graduation_remaining
tools_detail.description: Read 'mezuniyetime ne kaldı', academic status, and debts for a program.

tools_detail.name: obs_get_campus_card
tools_detail.description: Read campus card balance and recent transactions from OBS (requires login).

tools_detail.name: obs_download_transcript
tools_detail.description: Download OBS transcript preview PDF to the local state downloads folder.

tools_detail.name: obs_search_courses
tools_detail.description: Search the public OBS course catalog by code or name (no auth required).

tools_detail.name: obs_get_course_prerequisites
tools_detail.description: Query prerequisite and postrequisite relationships for a course from the OBS public catalog. Supports chain queries up to 10 levels deep.

tools_detail.name: obs_calculate_gpa
tools_detail.description: Calculate GPA/GANO from OBS registered courses. Supports projected grades for what-if scenarios. Uses İTÜ 4.00-scale letter grade conversion.

tools_detail.name: check_course_conflicts
tools_detail.description: Check for schedule time conflicts between courses by CRN. Uses the public course schedule to look up session day/time/room.

tools_detail.name: obs_get_notifications
tools_detail.description: Read İTÜ Portal notifications (requires login).

tools_detail.name: obs_get_help_tickets
tools_detail.description: Read İTÜ Portal help desk tickets (requires login).

tools_detail.name: obs_get_cloud_quota
tools_detail.description: Read İTÜ Mail and İTÜ Bulut storage quota from the Portal (requires login).

tools_detail.name: get_academic_calendar
tools_detail.description: Read the İTÜ academic calendar — semester dates, exams, holidays, registration periods (public, no login).

tools_detail.name: get_cafeteria_menu
tools_detail.description: Read today's cafeteria menu from the İTÜ Portal (requires login). Shows daily lunch/dinner meals.

tools_detail.name: get_public_course_schedule
tools_detail.description: Read the public OBS course schedule for a department. No login required — reads the open DersProgram page. Returns structured course list with CRN, instructor, sessions (day/time/room), capacity, enrolled count, and prerequisite links.

tools_detail.name: get_public_course_prerequisites
tools_detail.description: Read prerequisite details from a course's public OBS DersBilgi page. No login required. Returns structured prerequisite list with codes, names, groups, and types.

query.description: The text that the API compares page and database titles against.

anyOf.description: A set of criteria, `direction` and `timestamp` keys, that orders the results. The **only** supported timestamp value is `"last_edited_time"`. Supported `direction` values are `"ascending"` and `"descending"`. If `sort` is not provided, then the most recently edited results are returned first.

direction.description: The direction to sort. Possible values include `ascending` and `descending`.

timestamp.description: The name of the timestamp to sort against. Possible values include `last_edited_time`.

anyOf.description: A set of criteria, `value` and `property` keys, that limits the results to either only pages or only data sources. Possible `value` values are `"page"` or `"data_source"`. The only supported `property` value is `"object"`.

value.description: The value of the property to filter the results by.  Possible values for object type include `page` or `data_source`.  **Limitation**: Currently the only filter allowed is `object` which will filter by type of object (either `page` or `data_source`)

property.description: The name of the property to filter by. Currently the only property you can filter by is the object type.  Possible values include `object`.   Limitation: Currently the only filter allowed is `object` which will filter by type of object (either `page` or `data_source`)

start_cursor.description: A `cursor` value returned in a previous response that If supplied, limits the response to results starting after the `cursor`. If not supplied, then the first page of results is returned. Refer to [pagination](https://developers.notion.com/reference/intro#pagination) for more details.

page_size.description: The number of items from the full list to include in the response. Maximum: `100`.

tools_detail.name: API-retrieve-page-markdown
tools_detail.description:
Notion | Retrieve a page as Markdown
Error Responses:
400: Bad request
403: The integration lacks the read/update content capability required for this page.
404: Page not found or not shared with the integration.
429: Rate limited.

page_id.description: Identifier for a Notion page (or block) to read or update as markdown.

include_transcript.description: Whether to include meeting note transcripts. When false (default), a placeholder with the meeting note URL is shown instead of the full transcript.

tools_detail.name: API-update-page-markdown
tools_detail.description:
Notion | Update a page's content as Markdown
Error Responses:
400: Bad request
403: The integration lacks the read/update content capability required for this page.
404: Page not found or not shared with the integration.
409: Conflict (e.g. row limit exceeded).
429: Rate limited.

page_id.description: Identifier for a Notion page (or block) to read or update as markdown.

type.description: The kind of edit to apply. Prefer `replace_content` (overwrite the whole page) or `update_content` (targeted find-and-replace). `insert_content` and `replace_content_range` are deprecated.

anyOf.description: Provide when type is `replace_content`. Replaces the entire page content.

new_str.description: The complete new page content as enhanced Markdown.

allow_deleting_content.description: Permit deletion of existing child pages/databases that are no longer present in the new content.

anyOf.description: Provide when type is `update_content`. Applies targeted find-and-replace edits.

content_updates.description: Up to 100 find-and-replace edits applied in order.

old_str.description: Exact existing Markdown text to find.

replace_all_matches.description: Replace every occurrence of old_str instead of just the first (default false).

allow_deleting_content.description: Permit deletion of existing child pages/databases removed by the edits.

anyOf.description: Deprecated. Provide when type is `insert_content`. Inserts Markdown at a position.

after.description: Ellipsis-format selection ("start text...end text") to insert after. Cannot be combined with position.

position.description: Insert at the start or end of the page. Cannot be combined with after.

anyOf.description: Deprecated. Provide when type is `replace_content_range`. Replaces a selected range of Markdown.

content.description: New Markdown content for the range.

content_range.description: Ellipsis-format selection ("start text...end text") identifying the range to replace.

allow_deleting_content.description: Permit deletion of child pages/databases within the replaced range.

tools_detail.description: Emulates various features on the selected page.

networkConditions.description: Throttle network. Omit to disable throttling.

cpuThrottlingRate.description: Represents the CPU slowdown factor. Omit or set the rate to 1 to disable throttling

geolocation.description: Geolocation (`<latitude>,<longitude>`) to emulate. Latitude between -90 and 90. Longitude between -180 and 180. Omit to clear the geolocation override.

userAgent.description: User agent to emulate. Set to empty string to clear the user agent override.

colorScheme.description: Emulate the dark or the light mode. Set to "auto" to reset to the default.

viewport.description: Emulate device viewports '<width>x<height>x<devicePixelRatio>[,mobile][,touch][,landscape]'. 'touch' and 'mobile' to emulate mobile devices. 'landscape' to emulate landscape mode.

extraHttpHeaders.description: Extra HTTP headers as a JSON string object, e.g. {"X-Custom": "value", "Authorization": "Bearer token"}. Headers are included into every HTTP request originating from the page and persist across navigations until cleared. Pass an empty string to clear all extra headers.

tools_detail.name: evaluate_script

function.description:
A JavaScript function declaration to be executed by the tool in the currently selected page.
Example without arguments: `() => document.title` or `async () => await fetch("example.com")`.
Example with arguments: `(el) => el.innerText`

items.description: The uid of an element on the page from the page content snapshot

args.description: An optional list of arguments to pass to the function.

filePath.description: The absolute or relative path to a file to save the script output to. If omitted, the output is returned inline.

dialogAction.description: Handle dialogs while execution. "accept", "dismiss", or string for response of window.prompt. Defaults to accept.

tools_detail.description: Fill out multiple form elements (inputs, selects, checkboxes, radios) at once. tools_detail.name: search_papers
tools_detail.description:
Search for papers on arXiv with advanced filtering and query optimization.

QUERY CONSTRUCTION GUIDELINES:
- Use QUOTED PHRASES for exact matches: "multi-agent systems", "neural networks", "machine learning"
- Combine related concepts with OR: "AI agents" OR "software agents" OR "intelligent agents"  
- Use field-specific searches for precision:
  - ti:"exact title phrase" - search in titles only

query.description: Search query using quoted phrases for exact matches (e.g., '"machine learning" OR "deep learning"') or specific technical terms. Avoid overly broad or generic terms.

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 10, max: 50). Use 15-20 for comprehensive searches.

date_from.description: Start date for papers (YYYY-MM-DD format). Use to find recent work, e.g., '2023-01-01' for last 2 years.

date_to.description: End date for papers (YYYY-MM-DD format). Use with date_from to find historical work, e.g., '2020-12-31' for older research.

categories.description: Strongly recommended: arXiv categories to focus search (e.g., ['cs.AI', 'cs.MA'] for agent research, ['cs.LG'] for ML, ['cs.CL'] for NLP, ['cs.CV'] for vision). Greatly improves relevance.

sort_by.description: Sort results by 'relevance' (most relevant first, default) or 'date' (newest first). Use 'relevance' for focused searches, 'date' for recent developments.

tools_detail.name: download_paper
tools_detail.description: Download a paper from arXiv and return its full text content. Tries the HTML version first for clean extraction; falls back to PDF conversion if HTML is unavailable. Returns the paper content directly so you can read it immediately.

paper_id.description: The arXiv ID of the paper to download (e.g. '2103.12345')

tools_detail.name: list_papers
tools_detail.description: List all papers that have been downloaded and stored locally via download_paper. Returns arXiv IDs only — use read_paper to access content. Returns an empty list if no papers have been downloaded yet. Workflow: search_papers -> download_paper -> list_papers -> read_paper.

tools_detail.name: read_paper
tools_detail.description: Read the full text content of a paper that was previously downloaded via download_paper. Returns the paper in markdown format. Will fail with a clear error if the paper has not been downloaded yet — call download_paper first. Workflow: search_papers -> download_paper -> read_paper.

paper_id.description: The arXiv ID of the paper to read

tools_detail.name: get_abstract
tools_detail.description: Fetch the abstract and metadata of an arXiv paper by ID, WITHOUT downloading the full paper. Use this before download_paper to assess relevance and save tokens. Returns: title, authors, abstract, categories, published date, and PDF URL. Workflow tip: search_papers -> get_abstract (check relevance) -> download_paper (if needed) -> read_paper.

tools_detail.name: semantic_search
tools_detail.description: Semantic similarity search over papers you have already downloaded locally via download_paper. Supports free-text queries (e.g. 'attention mechanisms for long sequences') or finding papers similar to a given paper_id. IMPORTANT: only searches your local downloaded collection — will return empty results if no papers have been downloaded yet. Use search_papers to find papers on arXiv, then download_

query.description: Free-text semantic query.

paper_id.description: Find papers semantically similar to this arXiv paper ID.

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 10).

tools_detail.name: reindex
tools_detail.description: Rebuild the local semantic index for downloaded papers.

clear_existing.description: If true, clear the existing index before rebuilding.

tools_detail.name: citation_graph
tools_detail.description: Return papers citing an arXiv paper and papers that it references using Semantic Scholar's citation graph.

tools_detail.name: watch_topic
tools_detail.description: Save or update a persistent research topic watch. When checked via check_alerts, returns only papers published since the last check — acting as a standing alert for new work on a topic. The topic string uses the same query syntax as search_papers (quoted phrases, field specifiers, boolean operators). Examples: '"diffusion models" AND ti:"video generation"', 'au:"LeCun" AND cs.LG'. Calling watch_to

topic.description: Query string to monitor. Uses arXiv search syntax — quoted phrases for exact matches, field specifiers (ti:, au:, abs:), and boolean operators (AND, OR, ANDNOT). Example: '"reinforcement learning" AND "robotics"'.

categories.description: Optional arXiv category filter (e.g. ['cs.LG', 'cs.AI']). Narrows results to specific fields.

max_results.description: Maximum papers to return per alert check (default: 10).

tools_detail.name: check_alerts
tools_detail.description: Check all saved topic watches for newly published papers since the last check. Omitting the topic parameter runs ALL saved watches and returns new papers for each. Passing a topic string checks only that specific watch. Updates each watch's last_checked timestamp after running, so subsequent calls only return newer papers. Use watch_topic to register topics before calling this. Returns a summary w

topic.description: Optional: check only this specific watched topic (must match the topic string used in watch_topic exactly). Omit to check all saved watches.

items.description:
Response model for chemical compound information from MyChem.info.

This class represents the complete response from the MyChem.info API for a chemical compound.
It includes the compound's identifier, version information, and detailed PubChem data.

The response structure follows the MyChem.info API format and includes:
- _id: The primary identifier (typically InChIKey)
- _version: Version number of the data
- pubchem: Detailed PubChem information (if available)

For more information about the available fields and data sources, see:
https://docs.mychem.info/en/latest/doc/data.html#available-fields

anyOf.description:
PubChem information for a chemical compound.

This class represents detailed chemical information from PubChem, including structural properties,
physical characteristics, and identifiers. The data is sourced from the PubChem database and
provides comprehensive information about chemical compounds.

Key fields include:
- Structural information (SMILES, InChI, molecular formula)
- Physical properties (molecular weight, exact mass)
- Chemical properties (hydrogen bond donors/acceptors, rotatable bonds)
- Stereochemistry information (chiral centers, stereocenters)
- Chemical identifiers (CID, InChIKey)

For more details on available fields and their meanings, see:
https://docs.mychem.info/en/latest/doc/data.html#available-fields

chiral_atom_count.description: Number of chiral atoms in the molecule

chiral_bond_count.description: Number of chiral bonds in the molecule

covalently_bonded_unit_count.description: Number of covalently bonded units in the molecule

formal_charge.description: Net formal charge of the molecule

hydrogen_bond_acceptor_count.description: Number of hydrogen bond acceptor atoms

hydrogen_bond_donor_count.description: Number of hydrogen bond donor atoms

inchi_key.description: InChI Key (27-character hash of the InChI)

topological_polar_surface_area.description: Topological polar surface area in Å²

tools_detail.name: biothings_query_many_chems
tools_detail.description:
Batch query chemical compounds by multiple terms (e.g., names, InChIKeys).

Perform multiple chemical queries in a single request.

**Supported Usage:**
1. Multiple Query Types: ["C6H12O6", "C12H22O11"] (formulas), ["glucose", "sucrose"] (names)
2. Field Scoping: Search in specific fields using scopes parameter
3. Result Filtering: Return specific fields using fields parameter
